multiples.peerNote: LFMD flagged as distressed (0.8x EV/GP); clean peer band 6.1x-6.9x

growthAssumptions.note: User-override applied to all cases due to visible deceleration (Q4'25 +28% vs trailing 4Q avg 65%) and post-trailing-period FDA/DOJ/Novo regulatory actions Feb 2026

marginAssumptions.note: Bull capped at 76% vs mechanical best of 82%; reverting to 82% would require abandoning GLP-1 mix

capitalStructureFlags: Liquidity coverage ~1.7x; tightens to ~1x if Eucalyptus closes mid-2026 ($240M upfront + $710M deferred over 18mo)
Mitigant: $1B 0% convertible due 2030 with $70.67 strike + capped calls; no near-term refi wall
regulatoryFlags: FDA Statement Feb 2026 directly named HIMS re: GLP-1 compounding restrictions
HHS-to-DOJ referral Feb 2026 for potential FDCA + Title 18 violations
Novo Nordisk patent infringement suit Feb 9 2026 (compounded semaglutide)
Securities class action + derivative suits filed June-Aug 2025
FTC Civil Investigative Demand outstanding since Oct 2023
Cybersecurity incident Feb 2026 (PII compromised, no EMR access)
GLP-1 concentration: Hers brand ~40% of US revenue; GLP-1s primary driver within Hers
verdictCaveats: Material regulatory overhang not captured in trailing financials

fcfRealismCheck.deferralReason: FY25 capex/EBITDA distorted by acquisition spend ($1B investing outflow); regulatory binary outcomes unsuited to deterministic 5Y DCF; informal sanity check shows normalized FCF ~$200M+ supportive of base case

sources.freshnessNote: Q1 2026 10-Q pending (earnings May 11, 2026); re-run after release

notes: Single-segment per HIMS disclosure routes to Consolidated mode (not SOTP). Lifecycle classification of High Growth includes asterisk: revenue growth at 59% FY25 exceeds standard High Growth band but margin profile (just-positive 14% Adj EBITDA, expanding) sits below Mature Growth. Three user-override decisions logged in methodology section: (1) Y1 base growth 65%->12%, (2) Y1 bear growth 28%->0%, (3) Bull GM 82%->76%. All overrides justified by post-trailing regulatory actions and visible deceleration.
